revision_summary:
Four targeted fixes required before Stage 3 is production-ready:

1. LEAD-TYPE UNCERTAINTY: Remove or fully condition all assertions that treat this as a paid lead. The transcript contains no lead-type evidence. The schema explicitly flags lead_type as metadata Stewart cannot determine from the transcript alone. Every severity claim that hinges on paid-lead status must be reframed as conditional ('if this is a paid lead... if organic...') with the uncertainty flagged for the manager to resolve. Affects: multi_touch_patterns[0].stewart_read, cherry_pick_moments[4].stewart_read, coaching_focus[0].why.

2. MISSED MOMENTS — ADD THREE: (a) verify.interest_in_solar depth failure at 02:37 — Darla's unprompted motivation disclosure was acknowledged and dropped in two sentences; the schema marks this as the highest-weighted verify item with explicit depth_grading. This is a priority coaching gap Stage 3 omitted entirely. (b) Roof age boundary at 02:00 — 'about fifteen years' is at the hard_dq_if threshold; Holland never probes for replacement plans, missing both a DQ check and a roofing cross-sell signal. (c) Credit ambiguity at 06:17 — Darla's 'around that' response to the 670 threshold is a soft non-confirmation Holland accepts without probing; the schema's spencer_operator_note on stated-vs-pulled credit makes this a coaching gap.

3. SCHEMA REFERENCE CORRECTION: cherry_pick_moments[6] cites protocols.spouse_decision for the title-check at 02:20. The correct reference is verify.title_co_owners. The protocol never triggered (Darla is sole owner); Holland executed a verify item, not a protocol. Fix the reference and adjust the coaching_implication accordingly.

4. HANDOFF BRIEF: Mark handoff_brief.applicable as true (or conditionally true) and generate a minimal brief. The call ends with an open bill-follow-up that is functionally a tentative_appointment. The schema's analysis_directives.handoff_brief_generation and conditional_booking_detection both call for a brief in this scenario. Minimum content: Darla's bill range ($500–$700, all-electric), 40-year tenure, service-area check status (cleared but unexplained to customer), open text-follow-up with no time anchor, roof age at 15-year boundary (unprobed), credit response ambiguous ('around 670').

Secondary (non-blocking) fixes: (a) Remove the suggested coaching line at 01:22 ('pays off the most... full run') — it implies a payback-period claim that violates numeric_discipline. Replace with intent/emotion framing. (b) Remove or reframe the suggested line at 03:12 ('every dollar you're spending on electricity is a dollar solar can replace') — this is a savings-projection claim that may violate setter_scope_discipline. (c) Strengthen the flip-rejection reasoning at 03:06 to cite contemporaneous behavioral evidence (Holland's in-the-moment response pattern) rather than retrospective evidence alone.
